summary: Ran targeted regression for editorial map/import/chapter organizer changes: build OK, 34/34 relevant Vitest tests pass, but protected app routes fail at runtime due malformed auth redirect URL.

critical.issue: Runtime error on auth flow: 'URL is malformed "/sign-in". Please use only absolute URLs'. Blocks authenticated editor/preview E2E validation.

ui_bugs.issue: Navigating to protected routes renders Next.js runtime error overlay instead of clean sign-in redirect.

integration_issues.flow: Editorial map + chapter move/delete in authenticated workspace
integration_issues.issue: Could not execute browser E2E for requested feature flows because protected routes crash before login redirect completes.

action_items: Fix auth redirect URL construction for protected routes (absolute URL requirement) in guard/middleware path handling.
After auth redirect fix, rerun browser E2E on /projects/[projectId]/editor and /preview to validate editorial map rendering and chapter move/delete server actions end-to-end.
Provide test credentials remains optional after redirect fix if protected routes are still gated in this environment.
critical_code_review_comments: src/lib/auth/guards.ts and src/lib/auth/middleware.ts use '/sign-in' relative path; current runtime expects absolute URL in this environment/version combination.
Feature logic for synthetic index generation, editorial map panel rendering, and organizer controls is covered by unit tests and passes; blocker is auth route integration at runtime.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use local URL http://localhost:3000 for current container run. Build passes. Relevant tests passed: 28/28 (import/actions/preview/workspace/importer) + 6/6 (repositories/actions.import). Repro for blocker: open /projects and observe runtime overlay 'URL is malformed "/sign-in"'.
rca of the issue: RCA: auth guard/middleware currently redirect/protect with relative '/sign-in'. In this runtime, redirect target is validated as absolute URL, causing malformed URL runtime error on protected routes. Repro: navigate to /projects in browser (or curl); response redirects to /sign-in and UI shows runtime overlay with malformed URL message. Mitigation: build redirect/protect URL using absolute origin-aware URL construction for unauthenticated targets.
